rulings.Case Title: ALISA BAKER VS. EQUITY GROWTH ASSET MANAGEMENT, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 28, 2024 line 6. DEFENDANT EQUITY GROWTH ASSET MANAGEMENT DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The complaint asserts 4 causes of action based on two alternatives (the first two causes of action seek determination of rights and remedies IF all co-owners are bound by TICA; the second two causes of action appear to seek determination of rights and remedies if there is no agreement in place. The demurrer to the first two causes of action for declaratory relief and breach of contract is OVERRULED. Moving party does not cite any authority directly on point addressing the impact of a foreclosure sale of partial interests in properties that are subject to TIC agreements. Sain v. Silvestre (1978) 78 Cal.App.3d 461 is factually distinguishable. The demurrer to the next two causes of action for declaratory relief and accounting is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a viable cause of action as between tenants-in-common in the absence of a TIC agreement. Based on the allegations in the complaint and arguments in the opposition to the demurrer it appears that Plaintiff believes that in the absence of a binding TICA among all co-owners, Plaintiff and the other signatory to the TICA are entitled to its benefits (i.e. exclusive possession of "their" units) while all co-owners have a right to joint possession of D's (non-signatory) unit. Plaintiff cites no authority to support this theory. If Plaintiff wishes to seek determination of the parties rights and remedies in the complete absence of the TIC agreement, Plaintiff may assert such claims. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
